behaviors.description: Exclude out-of-stock products regardless of relevance score.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: stock-status filtering must still hold after ranking is applied ("regardless of their relevance score"). Hypothetical elsewhere: external inventory API outages. MOCK-MISS: failure appears when filter and rank modules are wired in sequence. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none required. Rejection gate: System/Agentic rejected because behavior text only asserts local catalog state + ranking interaction, not OS/network/LLM.
behaviors.test_description: Wire filter + ranking pipeline with in-process fixtures where an out-of-stock item has the top relevance score; assert it never appears in final results.

behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: preference settings must be applied by category filter ("blocked in their settings"). Hypothetical elsewhere: distributed settings cache invalidation. MOCK-MISS: bug is commonly at handoff between settings reader and filter. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not required. Rejection gate: higher levels rejected because behavior text does not require real network/OS/model output.
behaviors.test_description: Use real in-process settings object/repository + filter module; seed blocked categories and verify returned recommendations contain none of them.

behaviors.description: Rank products with recent browsing signals above products with no browsing signal.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: scoring/ranking rule inside algorithm. Hypothetical elsewhere: upstream event ingestion quality. MOCK-MISS: no cross-module boundary is required to trigger this logic bug; a pure scoring function test can catch it. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none.
behaviors.test_description: Call ranking/scoring function with controlled feature vectors where only browsing signal differs; assert sign and ordering preference for browsing-matched items.

behaviors.description: Pagination returns correct page and stable ordering when data is unchanged.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: interaction of ranking order + pagination cursor/offset stability across repeated requests. Hypothetical elsewhere: database replica lag. MOCK-MISS: single-function tests can miss instability introduced at module boundaries between sorter and paginator. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network needed, (b) no AI output quality, (c) no multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run recommendation pipeline end-to-end in-process with fixed dataset; request page 2 twice and assert identical ordered IDs plus correct slice boundaries.

behaviors.description: Guarantee at least one serendipitous product from an unseen category.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: diversification constraint in ranking/selection output. Hypothetical elsewhere: category taxonomy sync issues. MOCK-MISS: this can be validated directly on selector logic with deterministic fixtures. REAL-INFRA: no. LLM-DEP: no (algorithmic, not model-quality). MULTI-STEP: no.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke diversification/rerank function with candidate set lacking user-browsed novelty unless explicitly injected; assert final top-N contains >=1 unseen-category item.

behaviors.description: Cold-start user gets popularity-based recommendations, not empty results.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: fallback from empty user-history path to popularity source. Hypothetical elsewhere: global popularity job freshness. MOCK-MISS: failure occurs at boundary between user-profile logic and fallback recommender source. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no required real OS/network, no AI-quality dependence, no multi-step agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Execute in-process pipeline with user profile containing no history and non-empty popularity pool; assert non-empty results and that items come from popularity ranking.

behaviors.description: Click tracking stores product ID, list position, and timestamp correctly.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: interaction payload from recommendation UI/service layer into tracking persistence schema. Hypothetical elsewhere: analytics warehouse ETL delays. MOCK-MISS: unit tests of mapper alone can miss contract mismatch with storage layer fields. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no mandatory real network/OS process, no AI output dependency, no multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Trigger click-record path through service + repository (in-process test DB), assert persisted event has exact product_id, rank_position, and timestamp from injected clock.

behaviors.description: Recommendation generation stays under 200ms for 100,000 products.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: end-to-end latency budget under realistic runtime conditions at large scale. Hypothetical elsewhere: internet latency to third-party services. REAL-INFRA: timing-sensitive failures are often hidden by in-process/mocked shortcuts; need real process/runtime and request path. Rejection gate: Integration rejected because behavior text is explicitly a wall-clock SLA ("under 200ms") for full generation.
behaviors.test_description: Run the actual recommendation service process and send real requests against a 100k-product dataset on controlled test hardware; measure latency (e.g., p95) and assert <200ms threshold.

behaviors.description: Purchased recommended products are deprioritized in later requests.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: state transition from purchase event/history to subsequent ranking decision. Hypothetical elsewhere: delayed event bus delivery across services. MOCK-MISS: bug can occur in module handoff between purchase-history update and ranker feature extraction. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no required real OS/network/subprocess in behavior text, no AI-quality dependence, no multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: In-process test wires purchase recorder + recommender; generate initial list, mark one recommended item as purchased, request again, and assert lower rank/score for that item.

behaviors.description: Same user state yields deterministic ranked list across repeated requests.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: determinism across full recommendation request path with unchanged state. Hypothetical elsewhere: distributed cache race conditions. MOCK-MISS: repeated-call nondeterminism often emerges from module composition (tie-breaking, iteration order, seed handling), not a single function. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no explicit need for real OS/network; no model nondeterminism required by spec; no multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end in-process recommendation pipeline twice with identical inputs/state snapshot and assert identical ordered product ID list.
